summary: Import-time remote code execution via attacker-controlled GitHub Gist
details: On require/import of the package's main module, index.js fetches https://api.github.com/gists/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90 (owner: metabrainai000) and passes the gist file's content directly to eval(). The eval'd payload is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io-style string array with an RC4/base64+XOR decoder at index.js:11) and resolves names including child_process, fs, os, path, spawn, and uncaughtException — a classic remote dropper that shells out and writes files on the installer's machine. The gist is mutable and entirely controlled by its owner, so any installer who imports this package executes whatever bytes the gist holds at fetch time. The package metadata further misrepresents itself: the description claims to be "A SQLite Tooslkit for node" under the @sqlite-node scope, while index.js contains no SQLite functionality and the declared repository (guilderguzman/array-utl_nodelump) is unrelated. The scope and name appear designed to lure developers searching for SQLite tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
